title: Register an International Business Company
description: Set up an International Business Company (IBC) in Barbados.
summary: An International Business Company (IBC) is a Barbadian company that does its business outside Barbados. Common uses include holding intellectual property, providing professional services to overseas clients, and managing offshore investments.

To register an IBC, you need both a declaration from a Barbadian attorney-at-law (the standard company declaration form) and a Government Form 1 application. The combination is what makes it an IBC rather than a regular domestic company.

An IBC must keep its records in Barbados, hold board meetings here and have a local registered office. These conditions are what allow the company to benefit from Barbados’s tax treaties and IBC tax regime.

The application is reviewed by the International Business Unit alongside CAIPO. Police record certificates are required for every director and shareholder. The whole process typically takes longer than a standard domestic company because of the extra scrutiny.
who_can_apply: anyone who wants to set up an International Business Company in Barbados
a Barbadian attorney-at-law acting on behalf of the applicant

items: the proposed name of the company (and alternatives)
the type of international business it will do
the name and address of the proposed registered office in Barbados
the names, addresses and nationalities of every director
the names and addresses of every shareholder

items.name: Form 1 Application for a Licence as an International Business Company
Declaration form signed by your attorney-at-law
Police record certificates for every director and shareholder
Business plan
items.note: A short summary of the planned activities.

items.name: Photo ID for every director and shareholder
Proof of payment of the application fee

how_long: A decision usually takes 4 to 8 weeks.

options.heading: Through an attorney-at-law
content: A Barbadian attorney-at-law will file the documents with the International Business Unit and the Corporate Affairs and Intellectual Property Office.

contact.agency: Corporate Affairs and Intellectual Property Office (CAIPO)
contact.address:
Baobab Tower, Ground Floor
Warrens
St. Michael

assumptions: Fees ($750 BBD incorporation, $1,000 BBD annual IBC licence) are illustrative. Actual fees should be confirmed with CAIPO and the International Business Unit.
‘A decision usually takes 4 to 8 weeks’ is an estimate.
‘Police record certificates for every director and shareholder’ is general practice for IBC applications.
‘Hold board meetings in Barbados, keep records here, local registered office’ are general IBC rules but the exact wording in current law should be checked.
